description: Stable release: Shell→Python migration, 9 AI platforms, centralized registry
changelog:
**BREAKING: Shell to Python Migration**
- All `.feature/scripts/*.sh` replaced by Python equivalents (requires Python 3.10+)
- Directory rename: `multi-agent/` → `multi_agent/` (underscore)
- Command namespace migration: Claude uses `.claude/commands/feature/` subdirectory, Cursor uses `feature-` prefix

**New Platforms (9 total):**
- Codex (`.agents/skills/`) — OpenAI's CLI agent
- Kilo (`.kilocode/commands/feature/`) — commands-only platform
- Kiro (`.kiro/skills/`) — skills-based platform
- Gemini CLI (`.gemini/commands/feature/*.toml`) — TOML-format commands
- Antigravity (`.agent/workflows/`) — workflow-based platform
- Plus existing: Claude Code, Cursor, iFlow, OpenCode

**Architecture:**
- Centralized platform registry replacing scattered init/update logic
- Extracted `resolvePlaceholders()` to shared module
- Deduplicated `compareVersions()` to `utils/compare-versions.ts`
- Remote spec templates from docs repo

**Key Fixes:**
- Windows encoding: `_configure_stream` with reconfigure/detach fallback for stdout/stderr
- Windows subprocess: `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` on win32, `start_new_session` on Unix
- Template placeholder roundtrip (`{{PYTHON_CMD}}` resolution)
- CLI version comparison for prerelease versions
- add_session.py table separator tolerance for formatted markdown

**Quality:**
- 312+ tests across 17+ files (unit, integration, regression)
- @vitest/coverage-v8 support
- Executable spec enforcement (7-section structure)
migrationGuide:
# Migration Guide: v0.2.x → v0.3.0

## Overview

All shell scripts (.sh) have been replaced by Python scripts (.py). This affects any custom workflows, slash commands, or documentation that references the old scripts.

## Requirements

- Python 3.10 or higher

## What Changed

### Script Extensions

| Old Path | New Path |
|----------|----------|
| `.feature/scripts/*.sh` | `.feature/scripts/*.py` |
| `.feature/scripts/common/*.sh` | `.feature/scripts/common/*.py` |
| `.feature/scripts/multi-agent/*.sh` | `.feature/scripts/multi_agent/*.py` |

### Directory Rename

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `multi-agent/` (hyphen) | `multi_agent/` (underscore) |

### Invocation Change

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `./script.sh` | `python3 ./script.py` |
| `./.feature/scripts/task.sh` | `python3 ./.feature/scripts/task.py` |

## After Migration

Run `feature update --migrate` to archive the old shell scripts to `.feature/scripts-shell-archive/`.
aiInstructions:
When assisting users upgrading from 0.2.x to 0.3.0:
1. Search for files containing `.feature/scripts/*.sh` references and update to `.py`
2. Replace `multi-agent/` directory references with `multi_agent/` (underscore)
3. Always use `python3` explicitly, not just `python` (Windows compatibility)
4. Check ALL locations: docstrings, help text, error messages, markdown docs
5. Run `feature update --migrate` to remove legacy shell scripts

Note: Users upgrading from any 0.3.0 prerelease (beta/rc) need no file changes — only the version stamp is updated.
notes: Aggregated release covering beta.0 through rc.6. All file migrations were applied in beta.0 — shell scripts are archived to .feature/scripts-shell-archive/ (not deleted). Users upgrading from any 0.3.0 prerelease will see no file changes — only the .version stamp is updated. New projects get all 9 platform options via `feature init`.
